2026-04-22T00:52:13.7201830Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7202253Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7202579Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7202881Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203125Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203314Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203444Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203602Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203741Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7203926Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7204087Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7204311Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T00:52:13.7204514Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). The new `ContentUnderstandingClient(ContentUnderstandingClientSettings)` constructor introduced in 1.1.0 is not documented anywhere in the README.\\n- No sample file (`tests/samples/Sample*.cs` or `samples/Sample*.md`) exists for the `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` / ASP.NET Core DI integration pattern.\\n- The `AnalyzeUsageDetails` / `GetUsage()` feature is covered only in `samples/Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` but is not mentioned in the README\\u0026#39;s **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section listing or **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** section, making it undiscoverable to readers of the README.\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** / **\\u0026#34;Main classes\\u0026#34;** list does not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md` — EDIT**\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34;** section (after the API key subsection and before the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Using IConfiguration and dependency injection (ASP.NET Core)\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This feature is experimental and subject to change. Opt in with `#pragma warning disable SCME0002`.\\n\\nIf you use ASP.NET Core dependency injection, you can register `ContentUnderstandingClient` using `AddContentUnderstandingClient` and configuration:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn your `appsettings.json`, add a section for the client settings:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;Endpoint\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;(yourfoundry.services.ai.azure.com/redacted)\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Main classes\\u0026#34;** list inside **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;**, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`** — Settings class that allows creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration`, enabling ASP.NET Core dependency injection integration\\n```\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section, update the list to include:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* **Usage Details** — Retrieve billing and token consumption details after analysis using `GetUsage()` (see `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md`)\\n* **Dependency Injection** — Register the client with ASP.NET Core dependency injection using `AddContentUnderstandingClient`\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` — CREATE**\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file to back the new DI snippet:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding;\\nusing Azure.Core.Extensions;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Azure;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ContentUnderstandingSamples\\n    {\\n        // Method kept for snippet extraction only; not run as a live test\\n        public void CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration\\n#pragma warning disable SCME0002\\n            IHostBuilder builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder();\\n            builder.ConfigureServices((context, services) =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                services.AddAzureClients(clients =\\u0026gt;\\n                {\\n                    // Reads \\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient:Endpoint\\u0026#34; from IConfiguration\\n                    // and uses DefaultAzureCredential for authentication\\n                    clients.AddContentUnderstandingClient(context.Configuration.GetSection(\\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient\\u0026#34;));\\n                });\\n            });\\n#pragma warning restore SCME0002\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet name **`CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration`** defined in `tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` (via `#region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` / `#endregion`) needs a corresponding placeholder in the README.\\n\\n**In `README.md`**, inside the new \\u0026#34;Using IConfiguration and dependency injection\\u0026#34; subsection, insert:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n(followed by a closing triple backtick). The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the actual code from the test file\\u0026#39;s `#region` block.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo sections are missing; this change adds content within existing sections only.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — injects the `CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` snippet into the README placeholder. [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T00:54:43.1467228Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T00:53:15.589498874Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58468,\"title\":\"[ContentUnderstanding] Docs: Document IConfiguration/DI integration and usage details for 1.1.0\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] by `@chienyuanchang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe 1.1.0 release (2026-04-21) added two new features to `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`:\\n\\n1. **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`** — a new `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]` class that enables creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration` / `IConfigurationSection`, supporting configuration-based credential resolution and ASP.NET Core dependency injection registration via `AddContentUnderstandingClient()`.\\n2. **`AnalyzeUsageDetails`** / **`GetUsage()`** — an extension method on completed analyze operations that surfaces billing and token consumption details (document page counts, contextualization tokens, per-model LLM/embedding token breakdown).\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README\\u0026#39;s **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34;** section only shows `DefaultAzureCredential` and `AzureKeyCredential` constructors. The new `ContentUnderstandingClient(ContentUnderstandingClientSettings)` constructor introduced in 1.1.0 is not documented anywhere in the README.\\n- No sample file (`tests/samples/Sample*.cs` or `samples/Sample*.md`) exists for the `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` / ASP.NET Core DI integration pattern.\\n- The `AnalyzeUsageDetails` / `GetUsage()` feature is covered only in `samples/Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` but is not mentioned in the README\\u0026#39;s **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section listing or **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** section, making it undiscoverable to readers of the README.\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** / **\\u0026#34;Main classes\\u0026#34;** list does not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md` — EDIT**\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34;** section (after the API key subsection and before the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Using IConfiguration and dependency injection (ASP.NET Core)\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This feature is experimental and subject to change. Opt in with `#pragma warning disable SCME0002`.\\n\\nIf you use ASP.NET Core dependency injection, you can register `ContentUnderstandingClient` using `AddContentUnderstandingClient` and configuration:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn your `appsettings.json`, add a section for the client settings:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;Endpoint\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;(yourfoundry.services.ai.azure.com/redacted)\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Main classes\\u0026#34;** list inside **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;**, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`** — Settings class that allows creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration`, enabling ASP.NET Core dependency injection integration\\n```\\n\\nIn the **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section, update the list to include:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* **Usage Details** — Retrieve billing and token consumption details after analysis using `GetUsage()` (see `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md`)\\n* **Dependency Injection** — Register the client with ASP.NET Core dependency injection using `AddContentUnderstandingClient`\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` — CREATE**\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file to back the new DI snippet:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding;\\nusing Azure.Core.Extensions;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Azure;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ContentUnderstandingSamples\\n    {\\n        // Method kept for snippet extraction only; not run as a live test\\n        public void CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration\\n#pragma warning disable SCME0002\\n            IHostBuilder builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder();\\n            builder.ConfigureServices((context, services) =\\u0026gt;\\n            {\\n                services.AddAzureClients(clients =\\u0026gt;\\n                {\\n                    // Reads \\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient:Endpoint\\u0026#34; from IConfiguration\\n                    // and uses DefaultAzureCredential for authentication\\n                    clients.AddContentUnderstandingClient(context.Configuration.GetSection(\\u0026#34;ContentUnderstandingClient\\u0026#34;));\\n                });\\n            });\\n#pragma warning restore SCME0002\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet name **`CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration`** defined in `tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` (via `#region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` / `#endregion`) needs a corresponding placeholder in the README.\\n\\n**In `README.md`**, inside the new \\u0026#34;Using IConfiguration and dependency injection\\u0026#34; subsection, insert:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n(followed by a closing triple backtick). The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the actual code from the test file\\u0026#39;s `#region` block.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo sections are missing; this change adds content within existing sections only.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — injects the `CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` snippet into the README placeholder.
